ideation: This sequence mimics the action of holding and operating a video camera.  The robot will appear to be framing a shot, adjusting the camera's angle and focus.  The left arm will hold the camera steady, while the right arm will be used for minor adjustments. I'll ensure that the movements are smooth and controlled, reflecting the precision needed for filming. The lack of elbows will be compensated for with careful positioning of the shoulder joints and wrists.  In the first frame, the left arm is positioned to hold the camera steady at chest level. The right arm is slightly raised, as if ready to make adjustments. In the second frame, the right arm moves to adjust the camera's angle slightly upward. In the third frame, the right arm moves to adjust the focus slightly. In the fourth frame, the right arm moves back to the ready position. The left arm remains mostly stable throughout the entire sequence.

frames.beat: left arm holds steady, right arm readies to film
right arm adjusts the camera angle upward
right arm fine-tunes the focus with a slight twist
right arm returns to ready, camera held steady
